abseqr 0.1.0
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Analysing Nanobody CDR3 Sequences with abseqr

Compute per-position amino acid composition

Source: R/aa_composition.R
aa_composition.Rd

Calculates the relative frequency of each amino acid at each position across a set of aligned or length-filtered sequences. Useful for identifying position-specific preferences in CDR3 regions. Use extract_cdr3() to obtain CDR3 sequences first.

aa_composition(data, col = "cdr3", length_filter = NULL)

Arguments

data

A tibble with a cdr3 column (as returned by extract_cdr3()), or a character vector of sequences. All sequences must be the same length, or length_filter must be set.

col

Column name (quoted or unquoted) containing the sequences to analyse. Default "cdr3".

length_filter

Integer or NULL. If set, only sequences of exactly this length are included. Useful for selecting the most common CDR3 length before computing composition.

Value

A tibble with columns:

position

Integer. 1-based position within the sequence.

amino_acid

Character. Single-letter amino acid code.

n

Integer. Count of sequences with this amino acid at this position.

frequency

Double. Relative frequency (0-1).

Examples

fasta_file <- system.file("extdata", "example_nanobodies.fasta",
                           package = "abseqr")
seqs <- read_fasta(fasta_file) |> extract_cdr3()
aa_composition(seqs, length_filter = 12L)
#> # A tibble: 12 × 4
#>    position amino_acid     n frequency
#>       <int> <chr>      <int>     <dbl>
#>  1        1 A              1         1
#>  2        2 R              1         1
#>  3        3 D              1         1
#>  4        4 L              1         1
#>  5        5 Y              1         1
#>  6        6 Y              1         1
#>  7        7 Y              1         1
#>  8        8 Y              1         1
#>  9        9 G              1         1
#> 10       10 M              1         1
#> 11       11 D              1         1
#> 12       12 V              1         1
